INC-8064 slack intercomP1open 49d
H2Bet duplicate bonus grants (BetConstruct 503-after-commit causing retries) confirmed roo

H2Bet duplicate bonus grants (BetConstruct 503-after-commit causing retries) confirmed root cause quickly, but 50 days later still ~300 duplicate grants/week with no fix deployed. BetConstruct account-manager gap means zero external traction since 5 Jun. Rewards team only picked this up via Linear ticket (REW-176) on 9 Jul and immediately said no capacity until mid-Aug. Intercom ticket closed twice (26 May, 6 Jul by Andrea Azzopardi personally) implying progress with no fix shipped and no ETA ever given to partner.

Open breaches
  • A2/A4: no decisive fix after 50 days, Urgent ticket parked a month+
  • A3: minimizing language ('doesn't seem urgent') contradicts confirmed weekly leakage
  • B5: BetConstruct escalation zero traction, proposed leadership escalation not actioned
  • C10/D15: Intercom closed twice implying resolution while incident.io still open P1
Proposed / pending
  • 503-as-success workaround - rejected by partner
  • PR #122 idempotency fix - closed unmerged
  • PR #123 observability only - merged, no fix
  • REW-176 interim workaround - Urgent, blocked until mid-Aug
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-05-26T14:40
  • temp solution being arranged - not delivered, blocked until mid-Aug
  • no ETA ever committed for root fix
Timeline (5)
  • 26 May: root cause ID'd same day, workaround rejected by partner 28 May
  • 05 Jun: BetConstruct outreach dead-ends, no account manager
  • 11 Jun: confirmed ~300/week duplicates still occurring
  • 06 Jul: Rewards team says no capacity until mid-Aug; Andrea Azzopardi closes Intercom ticket
  • 09 Jul: REW-176 created Urgent, blocked by capacity
INC-12436 slack intercomP2open 9d
223+ Tuohi/Noppa players missed welcome offers due to a lifecycle-entry race condition. Pa

223+ Tuohi/Noppa players missed welcome offers due to a lifecycle-entry race condition. Partner told FT internally 8 Jul they were 'proceeding with their termination' over this — never reflected in incident.io. Fix (Kafka switch, 13 Jul) was explicitly 'step 1' only; new instances of the same defect recurred 14 Jul (see also INC-13893, likely the same underlying bug recurring on Tuohi).

Open breaches
  • B5: 2-day lag between termination signal (08 Jul) and severity escalation (10 Jul), risk absent from incident.io
  • A4: defect recurring 8 days in, after only deployed fix
  • C9/C10: 14 Jul recurrence report unanswered
  • D15: incident.io materially rosier than Slack/Intercom reality
Proposed / pending
  • alternative LC trigger (payment vs registration) - offered
  • PR #58 crash-loop fix - opened
  • synchronous deposit-listener arming (real fix) - only 'prepared', not confirmed deployed
  • Kafka integration switch - deployed but insufficient alone
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-02T18:12
  • last partner message unanswered
  • alternative LC trigger offered
  • verbal commitment to cover in 14 Jul 14:00 CEST call - not confirmed happened
Timeline (6)
  • 02 Jul: partner first reports (pre-incident)
  • 06 Jul: INC-12436 created P3
  • 07 Jul: true scope found, 223 users across whole window
  • 08 Jul: partner signals termination internally, severity raised P3->P2 two days later (10 Jul)
  • 13 Jul: Kafka switch deployed, 'step 1' only
  • 14 Jul: new missing/delayed offers reported, unanswered since
INC-12998 slackP2open 5d
frank-casino deployment stuck in CrashLoopBackOff. incident.io summary is blank. Entire 15

frank-casino deployment stuck in CrashLoopBackOff. incident.io summary is blank. Entire 15-message Slack channel contains zero human diagnostic content - only join/leave and bot messages. Original lead left the channel within 5h of paging with no findings; current lead (Giancarlo Laferla) also left the channel 14 Jul with the incident still open/unresolved.

Open breaches
  • D15: blank incident.io summary
  • B7: no visible prioritized action on a critical-path crash-loop over 5.4 days
  • B5: ~20h escalation-to-accept gap, undocumented lead handoff
  • D13: zero runbook/basic-check evidence anywhere
Timeline (4)
  • 09 Jul: alert fires, lead auto-paged, acknowledges then leaves channel same evening
  • 10 Jul: lead reassigned to Giancarlo Laferla ~20h after report, status -> Investigating
  • 12 Jul: bot nudge unanswered
  • 14 Jul: current lead leaves channel, incident still open
INC-13738 slackP2open 41h
IP-whitelisting change introduced ~6 Apr silently blocked unsubscribe requests for Betiro,

IP-whitelisting change introduced ~6 Apr silently blocked unsubscribe requests for Betiro, Rivalo, Leonbets since 7 Apr - undetected for ~3 months until an unrelated Intercom incident led someone to check. Technical fix merged same day (13 Jul), but no legal/compliance escalation, incomplete impact quantification (Betiro has none 2 days in), and minimizing language used internally. An environment sweep also flagged 3 more brands (Rocketship, Fairspin, Frank Casino) with the same broken config pattern, unresolved in the read window.

Open breaches
  • A4: ~3-month undetected compliance-relevant failure, no pre-existing monitoring
  • D12: no legal/compliance escalation for a multi-month opt-out failure across 3 gambling brands
  • C10: no confirmed retroactive remediation of missed unsubscribes; Betiro impact assessment not started
  • A2/A3: minimizing language, suspicious consent-event drop dismissed without follow-up
Proposed / pending
  • exempt unsubscribe endpoint from IP whitelist - done, merged 13 Jul
  • zero-unsub-event monitoring/alerting - in progress, not live, owner/threshold undecided
Timeline (3)
  • 06 Apr: IP whitelisting change introduced without forwarding-header exemption
  • 13 Jul: incident created after unrelated Lottomart ticket led to discovery; root cause reproduced same day, fix merged (PR #91)
  • 14 Jul: status -> Monitoring; zero-unsub-event monitor proposed, still mid-design

🔴 Needs attention 13

INC-11707 slack intercomP1open 13d
Betpipo partner reported repeated logouts from 26 Jun; two earlier fixes (IP-mismatch sess

Betpipo partner reported repeated logouts from 26 Jun; two earlier fixes (IP-mismatch session invalidation theory, AUTH_IP_BINDING disable) failed to hold and the issue kept recurring. Deep DB investigation 10-13 Jul found the real root cause: a shared UserTokens table where a player's re-auth can invalidate a CRM admin session on UserId collision. 4 fix PRs approved but not yet deployed.

Open breaches
  • C10: partner question from 10 Jul unanswered 5+ days
  • A2/A3: 'stabilized' framing repeatedly contradicted by recurrence
  • D15: status stuck 'Investigating' since 06 Jul despite active work through 14 Jul
Proposed / pending
  • disable AUTH_IP_BINDING - insufficient, issue recurred
  • scope token invalidation to IsAdminToken (4 PRs) - approved in review, not yet deployed as of 14 Jul
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-06-26T09:08
  • last partner message unanswered
  • multiple 'stabilized' claims (01, 03, 06 Jul) all contradicted by recurrence
Timeline (4)
  • 01 Jul: escalated to P1, first fix (disable AUTH_IP_BINDING) declared to stabilize, closed same day
  • 03 Jul: partner formally complains, reopened
  • 06 Jul: still recurring, reopened again, status stuck 'Investigating' since
  • 10-13 Jul: real root cause found (UserTokens collision), 4 PRs opened/approved, not yet merged/deployed
INC-12022 slack intercomP1open 12d
Cloudflare-origin security check (SEC1-216) blocked legitimate shortlink/unsubscribe traff

Cloudflare-origin security check (SEC1-216) blocked legitimate shortlink/unsubscribe traffic for multiple brands on 2 Jul; mitigated within ~2h, root-cause fix merged same evening. incident.io status lagged the real fix by days. Partner (Estelarbet/PLIN, Gloria Morocho) has been waiting 12+ days for a promised RCA report, with two broken 'update tomorrow' promises.

Open breaches
  • C10: partner RCA report 12+ days overdue, 2 broken promises
  • D15: incident.io status timestamps misrepresent when fix landed
  • A4: same CF/unsub-blocking pattern recurring across 3 incidents (also see INC-13738)
Proposed / pending
  • CF-origin carve-out - merged 02 Jul
  • file-serving revert - merged 02 Jul
  • deliver full partner report - still pending

  • first reported 2026-07-02T13:29
  • last partner message unanswered
  • RCA/report promised repeatedly, still not delivered as of 14 Jul; next promised 15 Jul
Timeline (4)
  • 02 Jul: CF-origin carve-out (PR #427) and file-serving revert (PR #62) both merged same day
  • 06 Jul: status moved Investigating->Fixing 4 days after code was already fixed
  • 10 Jul: status -> Monitoring
  • 13-14 Jul: two more broken 'update tomorrow' promises to partner
INC-13853 slack intercomP1open 26h
Partner (Rhino) raised a P1 believing FT/SOC had agreed to actively monitor a Pragmatic pl

Partner (Rhino) raised a P1 believing FT/SOC had agreed to actively monitor a Pragmatic platform release; FT determined internally within hours no such commitment existed. Engineers ran repeated ClickHouse checks, caught and corrected a premature partner claim that sync was 'all completed', and confirmed no platform issue by end of day.

Open breaches
  • C9: ~2h55m partner-facing silence (11:28-14:23 UTC) with 5 SLA-missed events logged
  • B5: L2 escalation page expired unacknowledged
  • C10: not formally resolved a day after conclusion reached
Proposed / pending
  • Align SOC/SRE monitoring expectations with Rhino - agreed internally 14 Jul, not evidenced as communicated to partner
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-14T05:43
  • align expectations on monitoring responsibilities - not clearly confirmed sent to partner
Timeline (4)

  • 14 Jul 12:03 CEST FT confirms no monitoring commitment was made
  • 14 Jul 15:03-15:05 UTC engineer catches partner's premature 'all completed' claim

INC-9820 slackP2open 28d
Fleet-wide RDS Performance Schema memory leak, root-caused with rigor and fixed via PR #41

Fleet-wide RDS Performance Schema memory leak, root-caused with rigor and fixed via PR #416 (merged 1 Jul), rolled out fleet-wide with staged reboots by 9 Jul. Solid engineering, but incident.io record silent 14 days (01-14 Jul) and two follow-ups (activity-manager resilience, RAM/swap alerting) remain unassigned as closure approaches.

Open breaches
  • D14/D15: 14-day silence in incident.io record while real work continued in Slack
  • A4: two follow-ups unassigned as closure nears
  • D13: attached runbook is for a downstream symptom, not the actual root cause
Proposed / pending
  • Cap Performance Schema memory (PR #416) - done, merged 01 Jul, rolled out by 09 Jul
  • RAM/swap alarm - proposed 25 Jun, deferred, still not live
  • activity-manager-go resilience fix - follow-up created, still unassigned
Timeline (5)
  • 16 Jun: created, escalated P2 after downstream P1 same night
  • 30 Jun: root driver confirmed, canary validated
  • 01 Jul: PR #416 merged
  • 06-09 Jul: fleet-wide staged rollout
  • 14 Jul: lead asks bot 'is this still open?', plans to resolve
INC-10045 slack intercomP2open 26d
GiG (Power Play's upstream KYC) event-ordering bug leaves stale block tags, blocking new-p

GiG (Power Play's upstream KYC) event-ordering bug leaves stale block tags, blocking new-player journeys. Root cause found same day, multiple re-syncs done, but incident.io was closed 6 Jul against the partner's explicit request to keep it open, forcing a reopen 10 Jul when a related recurrence (119/ONT origin) surfaced. Permanent fix (order by GiG's update_epoch field) recommended twice, no incident.io follow-up created.

Open breaches
  • C10: no incident.io follow-up for the update_epoch fix despite being flagged twice
  • D15: summary/fields not updated to reflect reopen and recurrence
  • C11: closed against explicit partner request causing an avoidable reopen
Proposed / pending
  • historical re-sync - done multiple rounds
  • GiG source-side tag fix - partial, ONT/119 still pending
  • update_epoch as ordering key in analytics-service - proposed, not tracked
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-06-16T12:16
  • notify once re-syncs complete - kept
  • evaluate update_epoch reordering fix - in progress, no ticket yet
Timeline (5)
  • 18 Jun: root cause validated same day, 722 users affected
  • 26-30 Jun: re-syncs, GiG partial fix
  • 06 Jul: closed against partner's explicit stay-open request
  • 10 Jul: partner reopens with new ONT/119 issue
  • 13 Jul: still ~8 new stuck accounts/week, update_epoch fix proposed again
INC-11079 slackP2open 17d
Segurobet Consent Manager queue stalled since 27 Jun due to partner's (BetConstruct/Betco)

Segurobet Consent Manager queue stalled since 27 Jun due to partner's (BetConstruct/Betco) Operator API 500s. FT notified partner within 9 min and chased repeatedly, but partner has never given a fix/ETA (admitted 9 Jul they're 'at a loss'). This is the 3rd segurobet/BetConstruct API-reliability incident in ~5 weeks, never escalated to account management. Partner chase stopped after 10 Jul; alert auto-resolved 14/15 Jul with no human verification.

Open breaches
  • C9: 5-day silence after partner admitted uncertainty
  • B5: no formal escalation to account/partner management despite 18-day duration and recurring pattern
  • C10: alert auto-resolved with no human verification
  • A4: 3rd partner-reliability incident in 5 weeks, not flagged as systemic
Proposed / pending
  • notify partner + supply failing examples - done
  • no FT-side workaround/mitigation proposed (unlike a prior May incident on the same partner)
Partner view

  • 'we'll check' (01 Jul) - no ETA ever given

  • 01-06 Jul: repeated chases, partner replies non-committally

INC-13176 slack intercomP2open 4d
AI-agent Bedrock tool-name validation error bricked a Campeon Nexto partner's conversation

AI-agent Bedrock tool-name validation error bricked a Campeon Nexto partner's conversation thread. Root cause found in ~4h, fix (PR #459) merged and deployed by 14 Jul, partner confirmed working and Intercom ticket closed same day - but incident.io still shows 'Investigating'. A second bug (get_segments context-window overflow) surfaced during verification, worked around manually, no follow-up tracked.

Open breaches
  • C10: incident.io stuck 'Investigating' despite confirmed resolution ~1 day ago
  • D15: second bug and its manual workaround entirely absent from incident.io record
Proposed / pending
  • sanitize Bedrock tool-call names - implemented and verified, PR #459
  • fix get_segments context overflow - not code-fixed, manual workaround only, no follow-up ticket
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-10T09:26
  • workaround communicated 10 Jul - superseded by fix
  • fix deployed 14 Jul - confirmed working

  • 10 Jul: root cause found ~4h after alert, PR #459 opened same day
  • 13 Jul: PR merged
  • 14 Jul: deployed to production, partner confirms fix works, ticket closed; second bug found and manually worked around
INC-13757 slackP2open 38h
Incorrect game mappings caused a player's bets to match the wrong game (0% challenge progr

Incorrect game mappings caused a player's bets to match the wrong game (0% challenge progress). Investigation found scope escalated from 'one player' to brand-wide (113-114 of 114 Campeon origins, ~67.5k mapping rows) plus a confirmed crm-api resolver bug also hitting gembet/wsm-casino. Two systemic fix PRs (#2509, #2510) opened same day, but incident.io hasn't been updated since creation - still reads as a minor single-player issue.

Open breaches
  • D15: incident.io not refreshed since creation despite scope escalating to brand-wide/multi-brand
  • D12: true scope/remediation status only visible in Slack, not the incident record
  • unresolved time-sensitive customer impact: stuck player's challenge nearing expiry with no confirmed retro-credit
Proposed / pending
  • merge PR #2509 (deterministic resolver) - open, ready for review
  • merge PR #2510 (reconcile endpoint) - open, ready for review
  • manual retro-credit for stuck player - flagged, not yet actioned
Timeline (2)
  • 13 Jul: incident created (one player), root-cause investigation same evening finds brand-wide scope
  • 14 Jul: crm-api resolver bug confirmed, PRs #2509/#2510 opened, ready for review but not merged; stuck player's challenge expiry flagged as time-sensitive
INC-13893 slack intercomP2open 23h
Tuohi LC39 players missed bonuses; internally flagged as a likely regression of the same r

Tuohi LC39 players missed bonuses; internally flagged as a likely regression of the same race-condition bug class as INC-12436 (same partner) before the incident was even created. Root cause found and code-verified, but the fix given to the partner (service scaling) was later found by engineers not to actually work - never communicated back to the partner. Zero incident.io updates posted since creation.

Open breaches
  • B5: 11h+ silent while 'Investigating', zero incident.io updates
  • A4: likely recurrence of INC-12436's bug class, not reflected in incident record
  • C10/C11: partner given an explanation later found incorrect, never corrected; 3 newly-reported players unaccounted for
Proposed / pending
  • enforce add-before-deposit event ordering - proposed, unimplemented
  • scale scheduled-action-manager replicas - implemented, then judged ineffective
  • PR #103 (Redis engine upgrade) - open, review disputed
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-12T10:51
  • last partner message unanswered
  • 9-player ID list delivered
  • scaling explanation given to partner - later found by engineers not to actually fix the issue, correction never sent
Timeline (7)
  • 12 Jul: partner first reports via Intercom
  • 14 Jul 08:17: internal side-conversation flags possible regression of INC-12436's bug
  • 14 Jul 08:20: incident created
  • 14 Jul 12:35: root cause found (lost-event race condition)
  • 14 Jul 13:13-13:38: partner given scaling explanation + 9 affected IDs; partner begins manual crediting
  • 14 Jul 13:19-13:54: engineers privately conclude scaling fix doesn't work, partner never told
  • 14 Jul 15:05: last human message, 'TBC tomorrow'
INC-13895 slackP2open 23h
Fusion API 401 errors blocked on-site notifications/Rich Inbox sends for Spinbet Terragon

Fusion API 401 errors blocked on-site notifications/Rich Inbox sends for Spinbet Terragon (CRM Activities/Lifecycles). Same alert family as INC-12617 (Fusion API 401, 07 Jul) recurring a week later — investigation suggests root cause may again be partner-side (end users unauthenticated on partner's front end), not confirmed or communicated back to partner. Partner proactively notified once, ~4h9m after alert fired; no reply, no re-chase, and a bot silent-close nudge went unanswered.

Open breaches
  • C9: partner notified ~4h9m after alert (08:24->12:33 UTC), well past the ~40min target
  • B5: silent-close bot nudge (18:22 UTC) unanswered, no re-chase of partner
  • A4: 2nd Fusion API 401 incident in a week (see INC-12617), same pattern not escalated to owning team
Proposed / pending
  • identify partner-side root cause (end-user auth, not FT notification consumer) - flagged, not confirmed or communicated to partner
Partner view
  • last partner message unanswered
  • will update when more information available - no follow-up sent as of last read
Timeline (8)
  • 14 Jul 08:24 UTC alert fires (Triage)
  • 14 Jul 09:22 UTC -> Investigating
  • 14 Jul 10:24 UTC lead (James Hodgson) accepts
  • 14 Jul 12:33 UTC partner proactively informed via forwarded Slack message, status -> Waiting on Partner
  • 14 Jul 12:42-12:46 UTC Daniel Lindengren flags likely partner-side auth issue, not FT's notification consumer
  • 14 Jul 13:12 UTC Nazareno takes over as Incident Lead
  • 14 Jul 18:22 UTC bot nudges 'no activity, resolve?' — unanswered
  • 14 Jul 21:05 UTC Gerard E. Pelayo joins channel, no message
INC-13917 slack intercomP2open 20h
Campeon (Lifecycle-driven coin crediting) delayed players' reward coins up to 2h on Sat 11

Campeon (Lifecycle-driven coin crediting) delayed players' reward coins up to 2h on Sat 11 Jul while notification emails went out promptly, generating a wave of player complaints; reported to FT via Intercom Mon 14 Jul. Engineers traced it same day to a real root cause: bonus 147/199 had auto-claim silently enabled via the Rewards UI on 9 Jul (no audit trail), and the auto-claim credit path serializes under load — 15 consumer pods performed like 1, capping throughput at ~13 msg/s and turning an 82k-message bulk campaign into a 2-hour drain. Partner Manager flagged internally this should be treated as P1 with the partner 'under observation'; incident.io stayed P2 throughout and received no status updates after creation despite the investigation.

Open breaches
  • D15: incident.io has posted zero updates since creation despite hours of substantive Slack investigation and a confirmed root cause — record not self-contained
  • D15/urgency mismatch: Partner Manager's internal note requesting P1 treatment ('product at risk') never reflected in incident.io severity, still P2
  • A4: partner note frames this as part of a recurring pattern of Rewards issues eroding trust, not flagged as systemic in incident.io
Proposed / pending
  • Fix serialization point in auto-claim credit path / batch or make async for bulk campaigns — recommended by Matteo, not yet actioned or ticketed
  • Workaround: fire plain Credit-VC action instead of auto-claim bonus for bulk sends — discussed by Rewards (Daniel/Linus), no decision taken
  • Add audit trail for Rewards bonus-config edits (root cause of 9 Jul flag flip is unrecoverable) — raised, not tracked as a follow-up
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-14T11:18
  • Rewards team engaged, findings to follow — not yet delivered as of last read, but framed as in-progress not overdue
Timeline (7)
  • 11 Jul 10:22-12:18 UTC: actual outage — 82k-message bulk reward campaign takes ~1h50m to drain instead of usual 15-30min
  • 14 Jul 11:18 UTC: incident created P2 from Intercom ticket; Partner Manager (Siria) internal note requests P1 treatment
  • 14 Jul ~14:00-16:00 CEST: Matteo (SRE) rules out DB/infra, traces root cause to auto-claim flag flipped on bonuses 147/199 on 9 Jul + serialized auto-claim consumer path
  • 14 Jul 16:54 CEST: Matteo pulls in Rewards (Daniel Lindengren) to verify and find a workaround
  • 14 Jul 17:22-17:52 CEST: Rewards discusses workaround (credit VC directly instead of auto-claim bonus) — no decision recorded
  • 14 Jul 15:34 UTC (Intercom): partner told FT has engaged Rewards team, findings to follow
  • 15 Jul 01:21 UTC: bot nudges again asking if incident can be marked resolved — no human response yet, incident.io still shows only the initial 'Investigating' status
INC-5630 slackP3open 75d
SMS distributor service has not processed queued messages for all Coinbar brands since bef

SMS distributor service has not processed queued messages for all Coinbar brands since before 30 Apr 2026 — over 1M messages backlogged, critical player notifications never delivered. Root cause (an external SMS provider outage/non-response) identified immediately, but 2.5+ months later there has been zero forward progress: FT has 'repeatedly requested status updates from the SMS provider' with no concrete feedback, and the channel is now purely automated (daily recurring per-brand alerts auto-merged in) with the Incident Lead rotated 8+ times among SOC/SRE engineers with no substantive handoff notes.

Open breaches
  • A4: chronic multi-month issue never escalated to a decisive fix or vendor-management track, just re-merged and re-monitored daily
  • D12/D15: the one substantive question raised (29 May, to Gustav) was never answered or tracked — record shows 6+ weeks of pure automated churn with no owner actually progressing it
Proposed / pending
  • Escalate to SMS provider for status — done repeatedly, no concrete response ever received
  • Gustav Tjäder asked to advise next course of action (29 May) — no reply ever seen in channel
Timeline (5)
  • 30 Apr: incident created, 1M+ message backlog across Coinbar brands, provider unresponsive since 'end of April'
  • 25-29 May: Andrea Azzopardi repeatedly asks lead (Matteo Rapisarda) for an explanation of why the incident is open and what SOC should do — no answer for 4 days
  • 29 May: Matteo finally explains — 'not much to do from SOC', pulls in Gustav Tjäder to determine 'where they stand' and 'next course of action'
  • 29 May onward: zero further human commentary in channel; only auto-merges of recurring per-brand SMS alerts and repeated Incident Lead handoffs (Nazareno -> Joachim -> James -> Matteo -> Simon -> James -> ...)
  • 14 Jul: Incident Lead reassigned again to James Hodgson, still no substantive update since 29 May

🟡 Watch 12

INC-13916 slack intercomP1open 20h
BrazilBet partner reported spoofed phishing emails using real player names/emails, asked F

BrazilBet partner reported spoofed phishing emails using real player names/emails, asked FT to check for a breach. FT treated as P1 security, made channel private within minutes, ran a live investigation (WAF/edge, DB/ClickHouse, CRM audit, SendGrid) and found no IOCs on FT's side. Partner updated 3x via Intercom; incident.io update posted before moving to Monitoring.

Open breaches
  • D13: WAF IP-block action item unconfirmed
  • D15: idle since 13:54 UTC 14 Jul, auto-nudge unanswered
Proposed / pending
  • Block 2 GCP scanning IPs at ambassador-brazilbet edge - proposed 14 Jul, no confirmation of completion seen
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-14T10:49
  • will reach out with new findings
Timeline (5)
  • 14 Jul 11:08 UTC partner substantive report
  • 14 Jul 11:19 UTC channel made private
  • 14 Jul 12:30 UTC WAF scanner-probe signal flagged, action item for edge owner
  • 14 Jul 13:57 UTC partner told investigation concluded, no IOCs found
  • 14 Jul 16:08 UTC status -> Monitoring
INC-10810 slackP3open 19d
12play/we88 (Momentum) players received activity challenges/rewards 45-50 min late due to

12play/we88 (Momentum) players received activity challenges/rewards 45-50 min late due to RDS overload — a previously known daily 4pm choke pattern (Giancarlo flagged it as recurring on day one) that finally got a real root-cause fix. Multiple engineers (Andrea Envall, Vincent Johansson, Simon Grenefalk et al.) ran a thorough investigation and landed four separate PRs plus two DB instance upgrades over three weeks, materially cutting RDS load. Handling was technically strong throughout, but the incident.io record has been static since 10 Jul and the Incident Lead is now on leave until 10 Aug with the final RabbitMQ/EKS maintenance window still unscheduled.

Open breaches
  • D15: incident.io status stuck 'Fixing' since 10 Jul, no update in the 4 days since despite active Slack coordination
  • A4: daily 4pm RDS choke was a known recurring pattern before this incident even opened, only now getting a durable fix
  • B8-adjacent: Incident Lead going on leave until 10 Aug with the final maintenance step unscheduled, handoff informal (in-channel only, not reassigned in incident.io)
Proposed / pending
  • ClickHouse migration for active-challenges metrics query - done
  • Content-variable reload-storm fix (PR #322) - merged 30 Jun
  • Rewards query split (PR #1914) - merged 2 Jul
  • crm-api caching (PR #2471) - merged 9 Jul
  • 12play/we88 RDS instance upgrade - done
  • RabbitMQ 4.x + instance upgrade with Momentum - tentative date, not yet executed as of 14 Jul
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-06-24T16:00
  • update 12play via Intercom on fix progress — discussed internally 30 Jun, completion not confirmed in Slack
Timeline (5)
  • 25 Jun 13:35 CEST: incident created after 45-50min fire-late reports; Giancarlo notes RDS chokes daily at 4pm, raised before
  • 30 Jun: PR #322 merged (content-variable reload-storm fix), custom PV deployed to 12play
  • 1-2 Jul: PR #1914 (rewards query split) and PR #2471 (crm-api caching) merged; 12play + we88 DB instances upgraded
  • 6-10 Jul: further RDS/RabbitMQ upgrade coordination with Momentum; Incident Lead handed Giancarlo Laferla -> Simon Brown (30 Jun) -> Daniel Lindengren (10 Jul)
  • 14 Jul: Daniel Lindengren departs on leave until 10 Aug, hands remaining RabbitMQ 4.x + EKS maintenance-window scheduling to Andrew Pirotta/Agustín informally — no incident.io lead reassignment
INC-12179 slackP3open 11d
llm-agent tool execution failing with 'failed to resolve container IP after 3 attempts' on

llm-agent tool execution failing with 'failed to resolve container IP after 3 attempts' on Daytona sandbox cold-resume, affecting meridianbet (and a merged duplicate INC-12203). Root cause confirmed 4 Jul with high confidence: readiness gate probes the control plane while the actual command hits the data plane, which hasn't reconverged on cold resume. Ranked fix filed as 4 follow-ups (real fix = data-plane readiness probe; band-aids = retry/backoff tuning, transient-error copy). No further activity since the RCA; bot nudge asking to resolve 3 days later went unanswered.

Open breaches
  • A4: 3rd occurrence of this recurring failure class (after INC-9483, INC-9919) — follow-ups filed but no confirmation any shipped before this went quiet
  • D14/D15: status stuck 'Investigating' since 4 Jul, bot resolve-nudge unanswered 7+ days, no evidence the real fix was actually deployed
Proposed / pending
  • probe toolbox data-plane in verifySandboxNetworking (real fix) - filed as follow-up, not confirmed merged/deployed
  • raise DAYTONA_EXEC_MAX_ATTEMPTS 3->5-6 + widen backoff (band-aid) - filed as follow-up
  • user-facing transient-error copy - filed as follow-up
  • reduce cold-resume churn via autostop interval - filed as follow-up
Timeline (5)
  • 03 Jul 13:19 alert fires, incident created P3
  • 03 Jul 15:36 duplicate INC-12203 merged in
  • 04 Jul 07:30 RCA posted and self-corrected same message, high confidence, mechanism confirmed
  • 04 Jul 06:52 4 follow-ups filed by Jeremy Curmi
  • 07 Jul 00:21 bot nudge 'no activity, resolve?' — unanswered as of 14 Jul
INC-12754 slackP3open 6d
Campeon Nexto users couldn't log in to the CRM due to a v2/v3 URL-version mismatch after l

Campeon Nexto users couldn't log in to the CRM due to a v2/v3 URL-version mismatch after login, compounded by browser cache. Root cause found and workaround (clean URL / different browser) delivered within ~15 minutes of the alarm; Patrik personally cleared the Cloudflare cache and restarted crm-api the same day and the fix held. The incident.io record has sat in 'Fixing'/P3 status unclosed for 6+ days since, with no further activity.

Open breaches
  • D15/C10: incident.io stuck in 'Fixing' P3 status 6+ days after the fix was confirmed working same day; bot resolve-nudge from 11 Jul unanswered
Proposed / pending
  • clean URL / different browser workaround - delivered, effective same day
  • Cloudflare cache clear + crm-api restart - done 08 Jul
  • core-xp escalation floated by Giancarlo - not clearly followed up
Timeline (6)
  • 08 Jul 14:25 incident created (P4)
  • 08 Jul 14:39-14:52 root cause found (v2/v3 URL mismatch), workaround given
  • 08 Jul 15:13-15:31 Patrik clears Cloudflare cache, restarts crm-api, issue resolved
  • 10 Jul 14:17 severity raised P4->P3 by Andrea Azzopardi, lead awaiting feedback if still happening
  • 11 Jul 00:33 bot nudge 'no activity, mark resolved?' unanswered
  • 13-14 Jul participants leaving channel, still open
INC-12909 slack intercomP3open 5d
Galabet partner reported players unable to claim reward bonuses despite meeting deposit cr

Galabet partner reported players unable to claim reward bonuses despite meeting deposit criteria. Investigation (Carla Santos, then Giancarlo/Matteo) found the underlying LC bonus-code action was configured/added a day after the qualifying player activity, and 340 Rewards bonuses were credited from the action but never claimed — pointing to a partner-side Rewards setup/Operator API issue rather than an FT platform bug. Matteo explained the caveat honestly to the partner (cannot guarantee full fix, may recur via Operator API) and proposed closing 14 Jul; partner still awaiting BetConstruct/Betco feedback. incident.io record itself received only one update since creation and the bot twice questioned inactivity, even though substantive work was ongoing in Intercom.

Open breaches
  • D15: incident.io record stale — only 1 update logged since creation, bot twice flagged apparent inactivity while resolution work continued entirely in Intercom
Proposed / pending
  • Correct Rewards/bonus-code action setup — recommended to partner, not confirmed implemented
  • Close ticket since no further FT-side action required — proposed 14 Jul, unconfirmed by partner
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-08T13:09
  • Rewards setup correction explained 10-13 Jul — fix not guaranteed, root cause attributed to partner/Operator API
Timeline (7)
  • 08 Jul 13:09 partner (Dilara, Galabet) reports players not receiving rewards
  • 08-09 Jul Carla Santos investigates, confirms trigger fired correctly but bonus not received
  • 09 Jul 08:31 escalated internally as P3 incident, Matteo assigned lead
  • 09 Jul 10:28 Matteo finds bonus-code action was added a day after player activity
  • 10 Jul 09:49 Matteo tells partner 340 Rewards bonuses credited but none claimed — Rewards setup likely misconfigured
  • 13 Jul Matteo caveats fix isn't guaranteed, may be Operator API/platform issue beyond FT control
  • 14 Jul 14:28 Matteo proposes closing ticket, partner has not yet confirmed
INC-13152 slack intercomP3open 4d
UW33 Malaysia partner's on-site notification CTA using javascript:LC_API.open_chat_window(

UW33 Malaysia partner's on-site notification CTA using javascript:LC_API.open_chat_window() to open live chat stopped working. Escalated to SRE via Intercom 9 Jul, incident opened 10 Jul. Internal Slack debate over whether JS-in-CTA-links was recently disabled platform-side was never resolved (engineers disagreed and left it inconclusive), yet Matteo told the partner the same day that no service-side change was found and suggested the issue was front-end/JS-handling on their side. No further activity for 3 days until a bot nudge (13 Jul) triggered a lead reassignment to Matteo; nothing since.

Open breaches
  • D13: internal debate on root cause (platform restriction vs front-end) never resolved before answering the partner
  • C10: idle 3+ days post-answer, lead reassigned via bot nudge rather than proactive follow-up, no partner re-check since
Proposed / pending
  • confirm whether JS-in-CTA-links is blocked platform-side - never conclusively confirmed internally
  • suggest partner check front-end JS handling - given to partner without internal confirmation
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-09T09:40
Timeline (7)
  • 09 Jul 09:40 UTC partner reports via Oracle bot chat
  • 09 Jul 12:58 UTC internal note flags for SRE escalation
  • 10 Jul 08:29 UTC incident created, P3
  • 10 Jul 08:44-15:16 CEST engineers debate JS-in-CTA restriction, inconclusive
  • 10 Jul 14:43 UTC Matteo replies to partner: no FT-side change found, likely front-end issue
  • 13 Jul 00:59 CEST bot flags no activity / ask to resolve
  • 13 Jul 12:17 CEST lead reassigned to Matteo, no activity since
INC-13220 slack intercomP3open 4d
Slott partner (Renat Likhachev) asked FT AI chat to enrich a push-notification stats table

Slott partner (Renat Likhachev) asked FT AI chat to enrich a push-notification stats table and deliver it as a downloadable file; the AI generated the full 448-row dataset but a widget bug truncated/failed to render it as a code block. The incident.io channel itself carries zero human diagnostic content (pure bot/escalation messages) — all real handling happened in the Intercom ticket, where agent Fábio Silva manually pulled the complete dataset from LangSmith and pasted it as text, then flagged it to SRE/AI Team as a P3 for a proper fix. AI Team confirmed root cause and closed the Intercom ticket 14 Jul with a fix-in-progress message, but incident.io itself has not been touched since the 13 Jul lead handoff and still reads 'Investigating'.

Open breaches
  • D15: incident.io record untouched since 13 Jul lead handoff, still shows 'Investigating' despite the partner-side issue being resolved and closed 14 Jul
  • B5: escalation to Jeremy expired unacknowledged on 10 Jul, only picked up via automatic workflow reassignment 3 days later with no visible follow-up
Proposed / pending
  • manual dataset delivery via LangSmith pull — done 10 Jul by Fábio
  • AI Team fix for large-output code-block truncation — in progress, no ETA
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-09T15:09
  • code-block truncation fix 'so it works better going forward' — no ETA given, confirmed 14 Jul
Timeline (6)
  • 09 Jul 15:09 UTC partner first asks Oracle bot for the AI-generated file
  • 10 Jul 08:15-13:51 UTC Fábio Silva manually pulls full 448-row dataset from LangSmith, posts as text, partner thanks him
  • 10 Jul 14:30 UTC Fábio raises P3 asking SRE to route to AI Team
  • 10 Jul 16:31 UTC INC-13220 created; escalation to Gérard then Jeremy (escalation to Jeremy expired/unacknowledged)
  • 13 Jul 01:36 UTC bot flags no activity, asks to resolve; 13 Jul 10:39 CEST lead reassigned Gérard->Jeremy via workflow, no further incident.io activity since
  • 14 Jul 19:58-20:10 UTC AI_Team (Justin) confirms fix in progress, closes Intercom ticket — incident.io left stale
INC-13664 slack intercomP3open 45h
SBTSolutions (Gergana Ramadanova) reported delayed bonus/activity-trigger processing after

SBTSolutions (Gergana Ramadanova) reported delayed bonus/activity-trigger processing after player registration on 11 Jul and again on 13 Jul, both traced to Activity Manager queue pileups under high event volume. Matteo Rapisarda root-caused it quickly, proposed a Kafka messaging-system migration to the partner on 13 Jul, then proceeded with the switch on 14 Jul morning without waiting for partner sign-off ('these guys are not collaborating... I can't wait for their reply'). The fix appears to be holding but incident.io's status and record have not been updated to reflect it.

Open breaches
  • D15: incident.io stuck on 'Waiting on Partner' since 13 Jul 14:13, not updated to reflect the 14 Jul fix; bot auto-nudge on 15 Jul unanswered
  • C11: partner told the migration would be scheduled/no action needed when it had already been silently applied hours earlier — record doesn't match reality
  • A4: same delay pattern recurred 11->13 Jul before the permanent fix landed
Proposed / pending
  • Migrate Activity Manager messaging system to Kafka (root fix) — implemented 14 Jul 08:44 UTC, appears effective, not yet formally confirmed resolved with partner
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-11T18:40
  • Kafka migration during low-traffic window, no partner action needed — told 14 Jul 11:50 UTC, but migration had already been applied ~3h earlier without disclosing that to the partner
Timeline (7)
  • 11 Jul 18:10 UTC — player enters lifecycles, activities fire ~17min late; partner reports via Intercom same evening
  • 13 Jul 10:09 UTC — incident created P3, Matteo assigned lead
  • 13 Jul 13:14 UTC — Matteo confirms root cause (traffic pileup) to partner, proposes Kafka migration, awaits approval
  • 14 Jul 08:16 UTC — partner reports a second, near-identical delay from 13 Jul
  • 14 Jul 08:20-08:44 UTC — Matteo switches Activity Manager to Kafka and scales pods, without partner approval ('can't wait for their reply')
  • 14 Jul 10:11-11:50 UTC — partner asks technical/scheduling questions about the (already-applied) migration; Matteo answers as if it's still pending
  • 15 Jul 01:15 CEST — bot auto-nudge asks if incident can be resolved; unanswered as of this read
INC-13931 slack intercomP3open 17h
G2 partner (Karina Edwards) disputed a Welcome-Offer bonus: player 556 got $42.79 credited

G2 partner (Karina Edwards) disputed a Welcome-Offer bonus: player 556 got $42.79 credited vs expected $70. Support (Fábio Silva) worked the ticket for a day before escalating to SRE tech team 14 Jul; Nazareno Scibilia reused the exact diagnostic playbook from INC-13171 (10 Jul, same bug class) and built a plausible 'slots-only NGR' hypothesis, but ~$3.80 of the discrepancy stayed unexplained. Lead handed over to Gérard E. Pelayo late evening 14 Jul with zero follow-up activity since; incident.io bot is already nudging to auto-resolve.

Open breaches
  • C10: partner told 'escalating to tech team' 14 Jul, no follow-up on the (inconclusive) finding since
  • A4: 2nd occurrence of same NGR/bonus-calc discrepancy pattern within ~4 days (see INC-13171), no permanent-fix ticket opened, handled ad hoc per-ticket again
Proposed / pending
  • slots-only NGR scoping theory - proposed, not confirmed (partial reconciliation only)
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-13T09:49
  • last partner message unanswered
  • escalate to tech team - done 14 Jul, no findings or closing answer sent back since
Timeline (6)
  • 13 Jul 09:49 UTC partner reports discrepancy via Intercom
  • 14 Jul 14:24-14:37 UTC support escalates to SRE tech team, incident created
  • 14 Jul 15:16-15:44 CEST Nazareno+Matteo reconstruct NGR via ClickHouse, reference near-identical INC-13171 case
  • 14 Jul 17:44 CEST slots-only NGR theory ($38.99) proposed as closest match, $3.80 gap unresolved
  • 14 Jul 23:37 CEST lead handover to Gérard E. Pelayo, no activity since
  • 15 Jul 05:21 CEST bot auto-nudge to resolve
INC-10902 slackP4open 19d
Recurring H2Bet nightly ClickHouse-writer queue pileup (RabbitMQ backlog + a payload type-

Recurring H2Bet nightly ClickHouse-writer queue pileup (RabbitMQ backlog + a payload type-mismatch bug on the Status field), consolidated into this 'DO NOT CLOSE' bucket incident since 26 Jun. Genuine root-cause work happened (a separate ClickHouse log-volume-full config bug was found and fixed via PR #435, merged 9 Jul; queue restarted/rolled 9 Jul), but the actual producer/consumer schema mismatch causing consumer errors has never been fixed, and 2 more matching alerts were merged in as recently as 12 Jul. A planned 'close Monday 13 Jul if stable' was never actioned — incident.io has had no update since 9 Jul, and the last human message (14 Jul) is a vague 'need to sanity check on this one, then ask them what they're doing'.

Open breaches
  • A4: root-cause payload type-mismatch bug (Status field) identified 26 Jun, still unfixed 19 days later; two more instances merged in as late as 12 Jul
  • D15: incident.io stale — no update since 9 Jul despite the promised 13 Jul close date passing and further recurrence
Proposed / pending
  • Fix Status field schema/type mismatch end-to-end (producer vs consumer struct) — proposed repeatedly since 26 Jun, still not implemented as of 14 Jul
  • ClickHouse log-volume/rotation config fix — implemented, PR #435 merged 9 Jul
  • Define sustainable autoscaling/throughput plan for clickhouse-writer at nightly peak — proposed 30 Jun/1 Jul, no evidence of implementation
  • Restart/reboot ClickHouse + roll clickhouse-writer pods, monitor and close if stable — done 9 Jul, planned close Monday 13 Jul — not actioned
Timeline (5)
  • 26 Jun 03:15 UTC — grouped under this bucket incident (absorbing INC-10603 + INC-10901), 28 alerts attached
  • 27 Jun — pileup recurs overnight, RDS scaled 4xLarge->8xLarge, clickhouse-writer scaled to 90 pods, Status-field unmarshal error confirmed
  • 9 Jul — separate ClickHouse log-volume-full bug found and fixed (PR #435 merged); CH pod restarted, plan to monitor over weekend and close Monday 13 Jul
  • 12 Jul — two more matching alerts (INC-13508, INC-13463) merged in, incident still open past the promised close date
  • 14 Jul 15:29 CEST — last human message: 'need to sanity check on this one, then ask them what they're doing'; no incident.io update since 9 Jul
INC-10938 slackP4open 18d
private — review directly
INC-13889 slack intercomP4open 24h
12Play (onetwoplay) partner reported a Promotion Calendar's Chinese comms profile mysterio

12Play (onetwoplay) partner reported a Promotion Calendar's Chinese comms profile mysteriously missing from the FT CRM backoffice, though the calendar itself remained live and visible to players (no end-user impact). Rewards team (Daniel Lindengren) found the root cause 13-14 Jul: a backoffice display bug affecting orphan-but-live door variations, not data loss. Partner was informed with a concrete fix ETA; two PRs opened 14 Jul and handed off to Sam Lundborg for testing 15 Jul.

Open breaches
  • D15: incident.io has no summary set and no formal Incident Lead role claimed despite active work by Daniel/Linus/Sam
Proposed / pending
  • backoffice PR - opened 14 Jul, pending test
  • rewards-backend PR - opened 14 Jul, pending test
  • handoff to Sam Lundborg to verify both PRs 15 Jul
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-10T04:15
  • fix release tomorrow/Thursday (14 Jul commitment) - not yet due
Timeline (6)
  • 10 Jul 04:15 partner reports missing Chinese comms profile on Promotion Calendar 3 (12Play)
  • 13 Jul 10:52 Rewards team engages after CS escalation
  • 13 Jul 15:06 Daniel finds data visible to players but hidden in backoffice
  • 14 Jul 07:53 incident created internally, REW-197 linked
  • 14 Jul 08:08 partner told root cause + fix ETA tomorrow/Thursday
  • 14 Jul 09:53 two PRs opened (backoffice + rewards-backend), handed to Sam Lundborg for 15 Jul verification

🟢 Under control 0

none
